summary: The patient likely has a diagnosis of Hypertensive Intracranial Hemorrhage given recent onset severe neurological signs without prior significant proteinuria indicative pattern matching classical presentation of Eclampsia; supported by imaging findings pointing towards trauma-induced vascular injury leading to focal brain swelling/bleeding event near surgical site. To confirm this hypothesis further diagnostic workup including MRI may be warranted especially focusing on detailed neuroimaging evaluation delineating exact extent & nature of bleed alongwith any secondary complications like edema etc., which could guide management plan tailored specifically addressing both primary cause as well as potential sequelae from such an episode requiring close monitoring due its life-threatening implications necessitating prompt intervention measures aimed stabilizing condition while preventing recurrence through appropriate pharmacological therapy targeting underlying pathophysiology driving excessive blood pressure elevations contributing substantially toward development of current complication state observed clinically today upon initial
